read: A concrete TileLang prepacked compact-row dKV producer now works: one CTA owns compact active compressed-group rows, consumes prepacked (token, selected) pair lists, reduces into a compact row, and writes the row without final global atomics. This proves TileLang can consume the prepacked route contract and avoid dynamic output-slot layout issues. It is rejected as the latency path because group-owned scalar contribution production gives up query-major q/dO reuse and scales badly at topk512.
decision: Do not pursue group-owned scalar TileLang dKV as the sparse-attention backward speed path. Keep this harness as a correctness/lowering reference for prepacked route consumption.
next_action: Implement query-major segmented TileLang/HIP producer: keep the fast selected-key contribution loop, use prepacked LI route metadata for lane/warp/block segmented accumulation inside the producer, and emit reduced compact rows or reduced atomics before the final small reducer.
